{"verdict":"request_changes","summary":"The new APIs are not at the natural Mathlib level: key definitions are either special cases where the general functor-of-points form is immediate, or vacuous subclasses that add no mathematical condition.","findings":[{"file":"TauCeti/AlgebraicGroup/Reductive.lean","line":42,"issue":"`AlgPoints` hard-codes only `k`-valued points, but the natural affine-group API is points over an arbitrary `k`-algebra `R`; base change to the algebraic closure is then just a specialization.","fix":"Define the general form first, e.g. `def AlgPoints (k A R) ... [Algebra k A] [Algebra k R] := A →ₐ[k] R`, and make `k`-points an abbreviation/special case.","evidence":"The added signature is `def AlgPoints (k A : Type*) ... := A →ₐ[k] k`, while the PR description explicitly needs base change to the algebraic closure."},{"file":"TauCeti/AlgebraicGroup/Reductive.lean","line":46,"issue":"The unipotence section assumes `[IsAlgClosed k] [AffineAlgGroup k A]`, but `IsUnipotent` only uses nilpotence of `((g : A) - 1)`, and the following theorem has to `omit` those assumptions.","fix":"State the predicate at the assumptions it actually needs, or separate a general `GroupLike` nilpotence predicate from any later algebraic-group unipotence API.","evidence":"Lines 46-50 introduce `[IsAlgClosed k] [AffineAlgGroup k A]` before `def IsUnipotent (g : GroupLike k A) : Prop := IsNilpotent ((g : A) - 1)`; line 52 immediately omits those hypotheses for `isUnipotent_one`."},{"file":"TauCeti/AlgebraicGroup/Reductive.lean","line":62,"issue":"`ReductiveGroup` and `SemisimpleGroup` are vacuous subclasses: their only new fields are `True`, so the classes do not encode reductivity or semisimplicity at any useful generality level.","fix":"Do not add these classes until the actual radical/unipotent-radical predicates exist, or make the fields state those predicates over the algebraic closure; then derive trivial special lemmas from that real statement.","evidence":"Lines 62-69 define `unipotent_radical_trivial : True` and `radical_trivial : True`, and line 72 merely re-exposes the `True` field."}]}

verdict_obj.summary: The new APIs are not at the natural Mathlib level: key definitions are either special cases where the general functor-of-points form is immediate, or vacuous subclasses that add no mathematical condition.

findings.issue: `AlgPoints` hard-codes only `k`-valued points, but the natural affine-group API is points over an arbitrary `k`-algebra `R`; base change to the algebraic closure is then just a specialization.
findings.fix: Define the general form first, e.g. `def AlgPoints (k A R) ... [Algebra k A] [Algebra k R] := A →ₐ[k] R`, and make `k`-points an abbreviation/special case.
findings.evidence: The added signature is `def AlgPoints (k A : Type*) ... := A →ₐ[k] k`, while the PR description explicitly needs base change to the algebraic closure.

findings.issue: The unipotence section assumes `[IsAlgClosed k] [AffineAlgGroup k A]`, but `IsUnipotent` only uses nilpotence of `((g : A) - 1)`, and the following theorem has to `omit` those assumptions.
findings.fix: State the predicate at the assumptions it actually needs, or separate a general `GroupLike` nilpotence predicate from any later algebraic-group unipotence API.
findings.evidence: Lines 46-50 introduce `[IsAlgClosed k] [AffineAlgGroup k A]` before `def IsUnipotent (g : GroupLike k A) : Prop := IsNilpotent ((g : A) - 1)`; line 52 immediately omits those hypotheses for `isUnipotent_one`.

findings.issue: `ReductiveGroup` and `SemisimpleGroup` are vacuous subclasses: their only new fields are `True`, so the classes do not encode reductivity or semisimplicity at any useful generality level.
findings.fix: Do not add these classes until the actual radical/unipotent-radical predicates exist, or make the fields state those predicates over the algebraic closure; then derive trivial special lemmas from that real statement.
findings.evidence: Lines 62-69 define `unipotent_radical_trivial : True` and `radical_trivial : True`, and line 72 merely re-exposes the `True` field.
